title: Youth Centers - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Youth Centers 23 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter No Filter Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness BOYS & GIRLS CLUB OF SOUTHWESTERN OREGON BOYS & GIRLS CLUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a positive space for youth to engage in fun and empowering programs, such as leadership and community service, after-school programs, academic enrichment, workforce training, performing and visual arts, camps, and personal health and fitness. 3333 Walnut Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Ages 6-18 Hours: -Varies by program -Teen Center hours: Monday-Thursday 2:45pm-6:30pm, Friday 12:15pm-6:30pm -Summer extended hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5:30pm Email (541) 267-3635 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF THE ROGUE VALLEY SUMMER PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers various summer programs including fitness, field trips, and outdoor activities. May include breakfast, lunch, and/or a snack. 307 W Wagner Creek Road Room 14 Talent, OR 97540 Eligibility: Grades K-12 Hours: After school hours: Monday-Friday 2:30pm-6pm Summer and Non-School Day Hours: Monday-Friday 7:45am-5pm Email (541) 292-4581 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER THE LOFT TEEN DROP-IN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. Offers a food pantry, help applying for state benefits such as OHP and SNAP, showers, laundry facilities. Also offers assistance with educational needs such as re-enrollment, school supplies, and specialized information and referral. Eligibility: Ages 11-24 that are homeless and unaccompanied by an adult Hours: Tuesday/Thursday noon-4pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY OUTREACH THROUGH RADICAL EMPOWERMENT ZEPHYR HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a space focused on building community and life skills for young adults. Offers a variety of workshops that are suggested by participants such as sewing, harm reduction education, resume building, and more. Eligibility: Ages 16-24 Hours: Varies by program Email (541) 870-0036 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONNECTED LANE COUNTY SPARK AT BOOTH KELLY DROP-IN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a youth drop-in center. Services include job finding assistance, resume and cover letter support, interview practice, career mentorship, assistance with completing college and scholarship applications, information and referral to housing and healthcare community resources, and access to shower and laundry facilities. 303 S 5th Street Suite 150 Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 Hours: Wednesday-Thursday 3pm-6pm, Friday varies by month Email (541) 654-4095 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DESCHUTES COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH THE DROP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a safe and supportive space for youth and young adults. Services include computers and wifi, charging stations for devices, games, music, movies, laundry, information on social services, coffee and snacks, and a quiet corner with activities. 1340 NW Wall Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 14-29 Hours: Monday/Wednesday 2pm-5:30pm, Tuesday 5pm-6:30pm, Friday 1pm-4:30pm (541) 797-3623 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES STATION 7 STATION 7 YOUTH DAYTIME CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center for young adults. Services include: wifi, charging stations for devices, games, music, movies, information on social services, and dinner. 931 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Runaway and homeless youth ages 11-20 Hours: 7 days per week 5pm-7pm Email (541) 689-3111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY COMMUNITY ACTION HOME YOUTH SERVICES HOME YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day shelter with drop-in services and an overnight emergency shelter. Supports youth with meals, laundry, showers, personal items, and community connection. Accepts school supply donations. Programs include: Runaway and Homeless Youth Program (RHY): Connects youth and their families to resources that meet their immediate needs. Also provides mediation and counseling resources for family reunification. The goal is safe exits from the streets, achieved through relationships with local law enforcement, street outreach, and working closely with the school district and other community partners. Youth Empowerment Program (YEP): Offered in 4 elements including internships and jobs, financial literacy, workshops and field trips, and extended support. Youth cohorts go through the program in 3-month cycles and can earn high school credit for their work and experiences during the program. Taylor's House is a 10 bed emergency shelter thay offers services for study support, social activities, job training. Onsite treatment and counseling services, as well as meeting rooms for appointments, visits and recreation. 625 Union Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 11-17, 18 if still in school or working towards a GED Hours: The Drop In: Noon-7pm / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 8am-6:30pm Email (971) 273-7300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HOMELESS YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps youth build positive relationships with adults and role models to improve their stability. Connects them with support services, including case management, cultural resources, mentors, mental health and addiction services, education, job assistance, as well as on-site food, clothing, and shelter resources. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are currently experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4:30pm Email (503) 288-8177 326 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPHELIA'S PLACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps girls make healthy life choices through empowerment, education, and support. Provides on-site after-school programs and individual, family, and group therapy. Also provides services in schools, including classroom presentations, girl support groups, and staff training in the areas of healthy relationships, bullying and harassment, healthy body image, and digital safety. Offers professional development training for people-serving organizations, nonprofits, and businesses. 1577 Pearl Street Suite 100 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Ages 10-18 who identify as female Hours: By appointment: Monday-Thursday 10am-6pm After School Drop-in hours: Monday-Thursday 3pm-6pm Email (541) 284-4333 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SRIPONYA COLLECTIVE MADRAS YOUTH DROP IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center with activities, food, education support, youth peer support, arts, crafts, and community support. 29 SE D Street Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Ages 11-17 Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 10:30am-6:30pm Email (541) 498-0968 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE HARBOR THE DOCK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. Offers opportunities to receive peer support, complete homework, and meet new people. Individuals have access to the podcast studio, kitchen with snacks, clothing closet, library, social activities like games, arts and crafts, book club, and movie nights. Event calendar is on website. Accepts gently worn clothing donations and books. 395 11th Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Ages 13-17 Hours: Wednesday-Thursday 1pm-7pm Email (503) 325-3426 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WINSTON AREA COMMUNITY PARTNERSHIP WINSTON TEEN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supervised out-of-school-time youth center where teens can participate in academic enrichment activities, including the Teen Cuisine culinary program, a learning garden, Science Action Club, a theatre/drama club, and the Youth Entrepreneur Success youth-run business. Also provides homework assistance, computers, board games, game consoles, and a pool table. A daily meal is provided through the USDA food program. Art program is available during the summer. 440 SE Grape Avenue Winston, OR 97496 Eligibility: Grades 6-12 or ages 11-18 Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 2:30pm-5:30pm, Friday 2:30pm-5:00pm (closed major holidays) Spring Break and Summer: Monday-Friday 2:30pm-5pm Email (541) 671-1274 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP YOUTH OUTREACH SERVICES YOUTH OUTREACH DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a youth center with access to academic supports, mental health supports, employment assistance, after-school activities, food such as snacks and staple items, clothing, hygiene supplies, and information and referral relating to in-house shelter services. 719 E 1st Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Ages 11-24 Hours: Drop in: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-noon Appointment only: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday noon-8pm, Wednesday 9am-8pm, (503) 472-0457 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP YOUTH OUTREACH SERVICES MCMINNVILLE YOUTH OUTREACH DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a youth center with access to academic supports, mental health supports, employment assistance, after-school activities, food such as snacks and staple items, clothing, hygiene supplies, and information and referral relating to in-house shelter services. 335 NE 6th Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Ages 11-24 Hours: Drop in: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-noon Appointment only: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday noon-8pm, Wednesday 9am-8pm, (503) 538-8023 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YMCA OF MID-WILLAMETTE CLUB Y Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Club Y is a monthly program where children socialize and play with supervision. Activities have a focus and include organized activities like arts, and crafts, games, tournaments, homework, science, computers, and fun with friends. Three hours max visit per day. 3201 Pacific Boulevard SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Varies by service (children must have parent or guardian on site) Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 926-4488 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUTH ERA CLACKAMAS DROP DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Drop-in center created by and for young adults. Provides opportunities to receive peer support, gain leadership skills, meet new people, set goals, and implement steps to achieve those goals. Support is provided via one-on-one peer support, support groups, and wraparound teams to provide youth/young adults with the tools to create change within youth-serving systems. Young adults can participate in leadership groups, find community involvement opportunities, use a computer lab, photo studio, and sound booth; and participate in social activities like games, open mics, and movies. 11097 SE 21st Avenue Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Ages 14-21 Hours: Monday-Friday 3pm-6pm Email (541) 214-6634 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUTH ERA COOS DROP DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Drop-in center created by and for young adults. Support is provided via one-on-one peer support, support groups, and wraparound teams to provide youth/young adults with the tools to create change within youth-serving systems. Young adults can participate in leadership groups, find community involvement opportunities, use a computer lab, photo studio, and sound booth; and participate in social activities like games, open mics, and movies. 1960 Sherman Avenue North Bend, OR 97459 Eligibility: Ages 14-21 Hours: Appointments can be made Monday-Friday 10am-2pm Drop-in center hours Monday-Friday 3pm-6pm Email (458) 209-1849 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUTH ERA MEDFORD DROP DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Drop-in center created by and for young adults. Young adults can participate in leadership groups, find community involvement opportunities, use a computer lab, photo studio, and sound booth; and participate in social activities like games, open mics, and movies. 246 State Street Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 14-21 Hours: Monday-Friday 3pm-6pm Email (541) 554-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUB OF SOUTHWESTERN OREGON BOYS & GIRLS CLUB BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF THE ROGUE VALLEY SUMMER PROGRAMS BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF THE ROGUE VALLEY SUMMER PROGRAMS BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF THE ROGUE VALLEY SUMMER PROGRAMS BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF THE ROGUE VALLEY SUMMER PROGRAMS CONNECTED LANE COUNTY DROP-IN SERVICES DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH THE DROP LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES STATION 7 YOUTH DAYTIME CENTER MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY HOME YOUTH SERVICES NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HOMELESS YOUTH SERVICES OPHELIA'S PLACE OPHELIA'S PLACE SRIPONYA COLLECTIVE MADRAS YOUTH DROP IN CENTER THE HARBOR THE DOCK WINSTON AREA COMMUNITY PARTNERSHIP WINSTON TEEN CENTER YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP YOUTH OUTREACH DROP-IN CENTER YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP YOUTH OUTREACH DROP-IN CENTER YMCA OF MID-WILLAMETTE CLUB Y YOUTH ERA DROP-IN CENTER YOUTH ERA DROP-IN CENTER YOUTH ERA DROP-IN CENTER YOUTH ERA DROP-IN CENTER
